llm.title: [GitHub] pytorch/pytorch: viable/strict/1774009733: [BE][Ez]: Add missing reserve values and moves (#177885)
llm.summary:
Add a few missing std::vector reserve calls to reduce allocation churn using Claude.
Add some std::max guards to make sure it doesn't underflow in the implicit conversion
Add a few missing move to std::make_tuple

Mostly authored by claude code
Pull Request resolved: #177885
Approved by: https://git

llm.title: [GitHub] pytorch/pytorch: viable/strict/1774006048: [xpu] Update torch-xpu-ops commit pin (#177721)
llm.summary:
Motivation
Update the torch-xpu-ops commit, including:

Add addmm, mm, bmm, baddbmm on SparseCsrXPU
Enable fused RMS norm forward and backward on XPU
Code clean up

Additional Context
These two ops (_fused_rms_norm_xpu and _fused_rms_norm_backward_xpu)  must be registered upstream in PyTorch, not in

llm.title: [GitHub] ollama/ollama: v0.18.2
llm.summary:
What's Changed

Add extra check to ensure npm and git are installed before installing OpenClaw
Claude Code will now be faster when run locally, due to preventing cache breakages
Fix to correctly support ollama launch openclaw --model <model>
Register Ollama's websearch package correctly for Op

llm.title: [GitHub] ollama/ollama: v0.18.2-rc1: launch: fix openclaw not picking up newly selected model (#14943)
llm.summary:
Sessions with a stale model field were not updated when the primary
changed, so the old model continued to be used.

ai-agent.title: [GitHub] langgenius/dify: v1.13.2
ai-agent.summary:
This patch release fixes several critical regressions and stability issues introduced in v1.13.1, including:

a severe regression in prompt message transformation that caused multiple LLM-plugin invocation failures across
LLM-related nodes, including LLM and Question Classifier.

frontier-tech.summary: Hi HN! We're Vincent and Jochen from sitefire (https://sitefire.ai). Our platform makes it easy for brands to improve their visibility in AI search.We’ve been working together for years and have backgrounds in RL/optimization at Stanford and software engineering. We came to this idea after speaking with marketing teams who were seeing declining traffic due to Google’s AI Overviews and didn’t know what to do.This space can feel esoteric.
